name: The Great Doward & King Arthur's Cave
terrain: Woodland paths, limestone ridge, some scrambling
highlights: Limestone escarpment full of caves, iron-age forts and peregrine nests above the Wye.
points_of_interest: King Arthur's Cave (Upper Palaeolithic); Little Doward Iron-Age hillfort; Seven Sisters Rocks
viewpoints: Symonds Yat Rock across the river; Coldwell Rocks
parking_start: Doward Quarry or Biblins car park (both free)
food_drink_nearby: The Crown Whitchurch; Saracens Head Symonds Yat East; Ye Olde Ferrie Inn
hazards_notes: Caves can be slippery; keep dogs away from cliff edges
